title: The Dark Pilgrimage
setting: October 1274, Carpathian Mountains and Central Europe
philosophy: The world does not scale to the party. The party scales to the world. Knowing when to run is a skill as important as knowing how to fight. The mountains are not a backdrop — they are a character.

levelProgression.milestone: Survived The Pallid Hart — Vladislav, Tomas, Gas Spore, the Letavec outside
Survived the Shepherd's Valley and helped the defector
Solved the Nachtmahr in Dolná Súča
Crossed Wolf's Pass, survived first Letavec contact
Survived the Erlking's forest with something intact
Arrived at Houska having survived the road
Resolved the False Hydra or the Penitent
Survived the Wild Hunt and solved the Wailing

encounterDesignPhilosophy.tier1_manageable: 30% of encounters — party can fight and probably win if they play well
encounterDesignPhilosophy.tier2_dangerous: 30% of encounters — victory not guaranteed, retreat always valid
encounterDesignPhilosophy.tier3_run: 25% of encounters — party cannot reliably win direct confrontation, survival is the goal
encounterDesignPhilosophy.tier4_do_not_engage: 15% of encounters — party has no business fighting this at current level

grants_xp: Surviving an encounter with something stronger — even if you ran
Protecting someone who could not protect themselves
Making a decision that costs something

acts.title: The Orava Mountains
acts.climax: Crossing Wolf's Pass and first direct Letavec contact
acts.theme: Learning that the world is dangerous and you are small in it

acts.title: The Road to Houska
acts.climax: Arrival at Houska having survived the road and the Erlking
acts.theme: The journey is the content — the destination is earned

acts.title: Houska and Bohemia
acts.climax: Resolving the False Hydra or the Penitent and leaving Houska alive
acts.theme: Nothing here is what it appears to be

acts.title: The Road to Čachtice
acts.climax: Finding the Letavec den and resolving its arc permanently
acts.theme: What you leave unfinished follows you

acts.title: Čachtice
acts.theme: The end of a pilgrimage is never what the pilgrim expected
